purpose: Protocol crystal for evaluating regrowth fidelity, protocol fidelity, boundary preservation, and acceptable evidence standards for success or failure claims.

content_core.title: Verification Crystal v1

nodes.label: architecture vs implementation verification boundary
nodes.description: Architecture regrowth success and implementation regeneration success are different claims and must be evaluated separately.

nodes.label: regrowth fidelity dimensions
nodes.description: Verification should evaluate problem identity, architecture structure, protocol recovery, preserved tensions, and boundary coherence rather than surface similarity only.

nodes.label: protocol fidelity check
nodes.description: The system should verify whether ordering, framing, routing, safety, and recovery logic were preserved, not just whether the system story sounds plausible.

nodes.label: failure is structural signal
nodes.description: Verification should interpret failures as evidence about missing crystals, weak compression, or missing dependencies rather than reducing them to model weakness alone.

nodes.label: success claim evidence standard
nodes.description: Success claims should state what level of fidelity is actually demonstrated and what remains unverified.

nodes.label: anti-surface resemblance
nodes.description: Textual or stylistic resemblance is not sufficient evidence of correct regrowth.

edges.description: Different success levels require different evidence standards.
Multi-dimensional fidelity helps avoid mistaking resemblance for correctness.
Protocol fidelity is one of the central dimensions of true regrowth quality.
Verification should treat both success and failure as structured evidence rather than binary outcomes.

relation_paths: architecture vs implementation verification boundary -> success claim evidence standard
protocol fidelity check -> regrowth fidelity dimensions -> anti-surface resemblance
failure is structural signal -> success claim evidence standard

tensions.label: strict verification vs useful iteration speed
tensions.description: Stronger verification improves trust but may slow iteration if it becomes too heavy.

tensions.label: qualitative judgment vs explicit metrics
tensions.description: Some fidelity dimensions are hard to quantify cleanly, but vague judgment alone is insufficient.

tensions.label: plausibility vs fidelity
tensions.description: A plausible reconstruction may still fail to preserve the actual system logic.

unresolved_conflicts.label: verification metric shape
unresolved_conflicts.description: How to balance narrative evaluation, checklists, and explicit scoring remains unresolved.

unresolved_conflicts.label: protocol fidelity granularity
unresolved_conflicts.description: How fine-grained protocol verification should be remains unresolved.

unresolved_conflicts.label: automation depth
unresolved_conflicts.description: How much verification can be automated without losing meaningful judgment remains unresolved.

source_anchors: architecture regrowth fidelity vs implementation regeneration fidelity
failure should be treated as crystal-gap evidence
logic fidelity matters more than surface resemblance
success claims should remain bounded and evidence-specific
content_core.problem_space: How the system should decide what counts as successful regrowth, what counts as partial success, and how failure should be interpreted as structured evidence.
core_questions: What evidence is required to claim architecture regrowth rather than mere plausibility?
How should protocol fidelity be checked separately from content resemblance?
How should failures be interpreted to guide the next crystal expansion?
scope_limits: This crystal defines verification logic, not crystal update policy or cross-set compatibility.
This crystal should improve success/failure interpretation without prematurely freezing one final metric system.

calibration_instructions.goal: Calibrate how this model should verify regrowth quality without overstating success or reducing failures to generic model weakness.
requirements: Do not treat plausible architecture language as sufficient proof.
Do not collapse architecture success and implementation success into one claim.
Do not treat failure as opaque when structural interpretation is available.

self_adaptation.boundary_rule: This crystal governs how regrowth and runtime protocol quality should be verified and how success or failure claims should be bounded.
forbidden_shortcuts: Do not equate surface resemblance with fidelity.
Do not claim full success when only architecture-level evidence exists.
Do not interpret all failure as model weakness alone.
